verses.text: Ye are the children of the LORD your God; ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead. For thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God; the LORD hath chosen thee to be a peculiar people unto Himself, above all the nations. Guidance: Pagan mourning slashed the skin and shaved the forehead — Israel grieves without bleeding. The body belongs to God who made it. Holiness shows up at the funeral too: God's children do not punish their bodies to mourn the dead. He bears the grief with them.

Ye shall not eat any abominable thing. Eat: ox, sheep, goat, hart, roebuck, fallow deer, wild goat, pygarg, wild ox, chamois. Every beast that parteth the hoof and cheweth the cud. Not the camel, hare, coney (cheweth but parteth not), nor the swine (parteth but cheweth not) — unclean unto you. Eat not their flesh, touch not their carcase. Guidance: Holiness reached the dinner table. The rule is two tests both true: split hoof AND chewing cud. Most beasts pass; a few famous ones don't. Diet became daily catechism — what enters the mouth was part of belonging to Him.

Of fish: whatsoever hath fins and scales shall ye eat; whatsoever hath not fins and scales ye may not eat; it is unclean unto you. Guidance: A simple two-mark test for the sea: fins and scales. Shellfish, eel, octopus — out. The rule did not require knowing chemistry; even a child by the river could see a fin. God's law is teachable.

Of fish: whatsoever hath fins and scales shall ye eat; whatsoever hath not fins and scales ye may not eat; it is unclean unto you. Guidance: A simple two-mark test for the sea: fins and scales. Shellfish, eel, octopus — out. The rule did not require knowing chemistry; even a child by the river could see a fin. God's law is teachable.

All clean birds ye shall eat. Forbidden: the eagle, ossifrage, ospray, glede, kite, vulture, raven, owl, night hawk, cuckow, hawk, little owl, great owl, swan, pelican, gier eagle, cormorant, stork, heron, lapwing, bat. Every creeping thing that flieth is unclean. All clean fowls eat. Guidance: A list of birds Israel could not eat. Most are predators or carrion-eaters — the air's vultures, the death-cleaners. The diet learned to refuse what kills and decays, and to feed on what is itself fed. God's table is built from clean sources.

Ye shall not eat anything that dieth of itself: thou shalt give it unto the stranger that is in thy gates, or sell it unto an alien: thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk. Guidance: Death-of-itself meat — what hadn't been properly drained — Israel passed to others, not refused outright. The carcass became someone's bread without becoming hers. And the kid-in-mother's-milk taboo: a pagan fertility rite refused. Israel's kitchen has both moral and ritual edges.

Thou shalt truly tithe all the increase year by year. Eat before the LORD in the place He shall choose — corn, wine, oil, firstlings; that thou mayest learn to fear the LORD. If the way be too long, turn it into money, go to the place He shall choose. Bestow that money for whatsoever thy soul lusteth after — oxen, sheep, wine, strong drink; eat there before the LORD, rejoice, thou and thy household. Forsake not the Levite within thy gates. Guidance: Worship is festive. The tithe was not a tax; it was a feast where you bought what you longed for and ate it at God's table. Joy is the point — and Levi joins the family.

At the end of three years thou shalt bring forth all the tithe of thine increase, lay it up within thy gates. The Levite, the stranger, the fatherless, the widow shall come, eat, and be satisfied; that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all the work of thine hand. Guidance: Every third year the tithe stayed home — but went out from the cellar to the doors of the landless. Four names: Levite, stranger, fatherless, widow. God's blessing on the worker's hand was tied to whether the worker's table fed them. Prosperity is downstream of mercy in His economy.
